steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: After the semicolon, the structure is subject (“this function”) with an embedded modifying phrase, followed by the blank, then a prepositional phrase (“from the biocompatible properties of these particles”). An independent clause requires a finite verb as its main verb.

steps.title: Apply the rule and choose
steps.content: A semicolon separates two independent clauses; the second clause must have a finite verb. “Stems,” a simple present-tense verb that agrees with the singular subject “this function,” serves as the main verb. Participles (“stemming,” “having stemmed”) and an infinitive (“to stem”) cannot function as a clause's main verb. That matches choice C — C is correct.
